data.title: Distributed Systems
data.description: Google's architecture is based on distributed computing, utilizing clusters of computers to handle vast amounts of data.
data.technical_spec: Microservices, containerization, Google Cloud Platform, Kubernetes.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Google uses advanced load balancing to distribute incoming traffic across multiple servers efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Google Cloud Load Balancer, Global Load Balancing.

data.title: Data Centers
data.description: Google owns and operates data centers globally to ensure low latency and high availability.
data.technical_spec: Tier 4 data centers, custom hardware, green energy usage.

data.title: Search Algorithm
data.description: The core of Google's architecture, continuously evolving with AI and Machine Learning.

data.title: Caching Layers
data.description: Utilizes extensive caching mechanisms to improve performance and reduce load times.
data.technical_spec: Edge caching, Google Cache, CDN networks.
